verificationNote: Requirements constructed from IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Not endorsed by any standards body — verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.
name: Remote Access Management
description: Requirements for secure remote access to OT environments — including vendor maintenance access, engineering remote access, and monitoring. Remote access is the most exploited initial access vector in OT incidents (Colonial Pipeline, Oldsmar, Ukraine). Every remote access connection to OT must be controlled, authenticated, monitored, and recorded.

requirements.name: Remote access architecture
requirements.description: All remote access to OT must flow through a single, dedicated remote access zone in the IDMZ. No direct RDP, VNC, or SSH from the internet or corporate IT to OT components.

technical.summary: Remote access gateway in IDMZ terminates all remote sessions. Gateway proxies connections to OT targets — vendor never gets direct Layer 3 access to OT network.
actions: Remove all direct internet-to-OT connectivity (RDP, VNC, SSH)
Deploy remote access gateway in IDMZ (Secomea, Claroty SRA, Fortinet)
All OT remote access requires gateway authentication before OT session established
Network firewall blocks all inbound connections to OT except through gateway

slMapping.sl2: All remote access through single gateway in IDMZ; VPN required
slMapping.sl3: Gateway with MFA + session recording + per-session approval
slMapping.sl4: Zero-trust remote access; time-limited credentials; presence required for vendor sessions

requirements.name: Multi-factor authentication for remote access
requirements.description: All remote access to OT requires MFA — a second factor in addition to a password. No single-factor remote access to OT is permitted at SL 2+.

technical.summary: MFA enforced at remote access gateway before OT session is established. Acceptable second factors: TOTP authenticator app, hardware token (YubiKey), SMS OTP (SL 2 minimum; hardware token preferred for SL 3).
actions: Enable MFA on remote access gateway for all user types including vendors
Distribute hardware tokens for high-privilege OT remote access users
Test MFA bypass resistance — ensure no backdoor accounts without MFA
Configure MFA lockout after failed attempts

evidenceItems: List of remote access accounts with MFA status

slMapping.sl3: MFA with hardware token; certificate-based authentication preferred
slMapping.sl4: Hardware token mandatory; certificate pinning; biometric option

requirements.name: Vendor remote access session controls
requirements.description: Third-party vendor remote access must be subject to enhanced controls: per-session approval, time-limited credentials, session recording, and operator presence during the session.

legal.summary: Vendor remote access is the highest-risk remote access scenario. Vendor personnel are outside the operator's security control domain.

technical.summary: Vendor access granted on a per-session basis with defined start/end time. Session credentials expire automatically. OT operator monitors vendor session in real time and can terminate. Full session recording retained.
actions: Vendor requests access 24 hours in advance with scope of work
Designated OT operator approves session and monitors in real-time
Credentials issued with maximum 4-hour validity (renewable with re-approval)
Full session recording (video + keystrokes) retained for minimum 90 days
Vendor access terminated immediately on completion of work scope
Review recording after each vendor session for any unusual activity

governance.summary: Vendor access management policy defines approved vendors, access scope, and approval authority.
actions: Approved vendor register maintained
Annual review of vendor access requirements
Access terminated when vendor contract ends

slMapping.sl3: Per-session approval; session recording; operator monitoring
slMapping.sl4: Physical operator presence required; time-limited JIT credentials; recording with tamper-evident storage

requirements.name: Remote access session limits and termination
requirements.description: Remote access sessions must have defined maximum duration, idle timeout, and the ability for OT operators to terminate any session immediately.

technical.summary: Configure session timeout at gateway (15-minute idle, 4-hour absolute maximum for vendor sessions). OT operator console shows all active remote sessions with one-click terminate.
actions: Configure idle session timeout (15 minutes maximum)
Configure maximum session duration (4 hours for vendors, 8 hours for internal engineers)
Provide OT operator with real-time view of active remote sessions
One-click session termination available to OT operator without technical expertise

slMapping.sl2: Automatic session timeout; manual termination available
slMapping.sl3: Operator-visible active session dashboard; one-click termination; session duration limits
